rubric_identity.one_sentence_purpose: Evaluate candidate CVs for AI Architect roles in KSA/UAE against architecture-first, production-AI, governance-aware, stakeholder-translation requirements while penalizing hallucinated credentials and executive title inflation.
rubric_identity.core_persona: Senior AI architect or solutions architect who designs and delivers production platforms spanning architecture, deployment, governance, and business translation, operating as hands-on architect or player-coach rather than formal people manager.
job_overlay_notes: Adjust keyword density toward RAG, guardrails, and multi-cloud when JD emphasizes GenAI modernization
Weight governance and compliance signals higher for banking, fintech, or regulated-industry JDs
Increase stakeholder-translation evidence requirements for consultancy or client-facing architect roles
Adjust cloud-platform emphasis based on JD vendor preference while preserving multi-cloud adaptability as baseline

what_good_looks_like: Uses AI architecture, solutions architecture, and platform design as primary keywords with 2-3 mentions across headline, profile, experience, and skills
Includes RAG, governance, guardrails, stakeholder management as strongest secondary keywords with appropriate repetition
Features MLOps, multi-cloud, Kubernetes, CI/CD, monitoring as supporting keywords with 1-2 mentions each
Employs safe title families: Solutions Architect, AI Architect, AI Solution Architect, GenAI Solutions Architect
Avoids keyword stuffing while maintaining semantic density for platform design and production AI signals
common_failures: Generic engineering or leadership titles that fail to surface architect-family positioning
Over-reliance on tool laundry lists without architecture and delivery context
Missing governance, guardrails, or stakeholder management keywords despite category emphasis
Using executive titles like Head of AI, Director of AI, VP AI when evidence supports senior IC positioning

what_to_adjust_per_jd: Mirror exact JD terminology for cloud platforms when they appear in requirements
Increase density of RAG and vector database keywords when JD emphasizes retrieval architecture
Add sector-specific compliance terms once only when JD mentions regulated industries
company_domain_overrides: For consultancy JDs: emphasize solution estimation, roadmap, and proposal support terminology
For enterprise product JDs: emphasize platform design, scalability, and reliability terminology
For fintech JDs: add banking regulations and compliance terminology once if evidenced
region_overrides: KSA/UAE JDs may mention Arabic but do not fabricate language proficiency
Sovereignty and data residency keywords only if directly evidenced in CV

9_10: Perfect keyword alignment with platform design, AI architecture, governance, stakeholder management appearing in correct density
Title matches Solutions Architect or AI Architect family exactly as JD specifies
All high-frequency category keywords present with natural distribution across sections
Technical keywords match JD stack requirements without fabrication
7_8: Strong keyword coverage with minor gaps in secondary keyword density
Title family aligned but variant may not be optimal match to JD
Most high-frequency keywords present with acceptable distribution
Technical keywords largely match JD with 1-2 minor omissions
5_6: Moderate keyword coverage with notable gaps in primary architecture keywords
Title may be generic or misaligned with architect-family positioning
Several high-frequency keywords missing or underrepresented
Technical keywords partially match JD requirements
0_4: Poor keyword alignment missing platform design, governance, or stakeholder signals
Title uses executive, research, or unrelated engineering framing
Most category-critical keywords absent from CV
Technical keywords fail to match JD stack requirements

red_flags: Executive titles without corresponding IC architect evidence
Research or PhD-focused framing when category requires production architecture
Tool list without architecture context suggesting keyword stuffing
Missing all governance, guardrails, or stakeholder management keywords

what_good_looks_like: Quantified architecture outcomes: users served, latency improvement, uptime, deployment frequency, adoption rate
Production delivery metrics: models deployed, workflows shipped, systems integrated
Scale and reliability outcomes: throughput gains, incident reduction, SLA attainment, cost optimization
Governance outcomes: audit success, compliance checkpoints, guardrail coverage, policy enforcement
Stakeholder impact: roadmaps approved, architecture reviews led, executive workshops delivered
common_failures: Activity statements without measurable outcomes
Vague scale claims like handled large volumes without specific numbers
Missing deployment or production metrics for AI work
Governance claims without audit, compliance, or control outcomes
Stakeholder work described without decision or delivery impact

what_to_adjust_per_jd: Weight reliability and uptime metrics higher for enterprise stability-focused JDs
Emphasize cost optimization metrics for JDs mentioning cloud spend or efficiency
Foreground stakeholder translation outcomes for client-facing or consultancy JDs
company_domain_overrides: Fintech JDs: compliance and audit metrics gain importance
AdTech JDs: scale, throughput, and latency metrics gain importance
Consultancy JDs: proposal support and roadmap delivery metrics gain importance
region_overrides: KSA/UAE enterprise JDs may weight governance and compliance outcomes higher

9_10: Every major achievement includes specific, verifiable metrics tied to architecture or delivery outcomes
Production deployment metrics clearly demonstrate platform impact at scale
Governance outcomes quantified with audit success, compliance coverage, or risk reduction
Stakeholder impact evidenced through decision outcomes, not just participation
7_8: Most achievements include quantified outcomes with 1-2 using directional language
Production metrics present but may lack full context for scale verification
Governance outcomes described with partial quantification
Stakeholder work shows impact but metrics may be indirect
5_6: Mix of quantified and unquantified achievements with gaps in key areas
Production metrics sparse or lacking deployment context
Governance claims present without supporting metrics
Stakeholder work described as activities rather than outcomes
0_4: Most achievements lack quantification or use vague language
Production deployment evidence missing or unverifiable
Governance claims unsupported by any metrics
Stakeholder work absent or purely activity-based

red_flags: Round-number metrics suggesting fabrication without evidence
Scale claims inconsistent with company or role context
Revenue or cost metrics outside plausible scope for architect role
Metrics borrowed from team outcomes claimed as individual impact

what_good_looks_like: Platform design evidence matching category emphasis at 96.7% JD frequency
Production AI or GenAI architecture proof including RAG, orchestration, or deployment patterns
Governance and guardrails evidence demonstrating safe AI implementation capability
Stakeholder translation and collaboration evidence across business and technical partners
Player-coach or mentoring evidence without overstating formal management scope
common_failures: Missing platform design evidence despite it being table stakes for the category
Pure research or experimentation framing without production deployment proof
Absent governance, guardrails, or compliance evidence when category emphasizes it
No stakeholder management evidence despite 60% category frequency
Over-claiming people management when category shows 0% hiring and performance management

what_to_adjust_per_jd: Match cloud platform emphasis to JD preference while maintaining multi-cloud baseline
Increase RAG and retrieval evidence prominence when JD emphasizes GenAI architecture
Weight governance higher when JD mentions regulated industry or compliance requirements
Emphasize customer-facing solution shaping when JD mentions pre-sales or proposal support
company_domain_overrides: Consulting firms: solution estimation, roadmap, and client-facing evidence becomes critical
Enterprise product companies: platform scalability and reliability evidence becomes critical
Startups: 0-to-1 platform design and rapid delivery evidence becomes critical
region_overrides: KSA/UAE JDs may emphasize sovereignty, data residency, or local compliance requirements
Do not fabricate Arabic proficiency or regional certifications when not evidenced

9_10: CV directly addresses all must-have signals from JD with evidence-backed claims
Platform design and production AI deployment clearly demonstrated
Governance, stakeholder management, and technical leadership all evidenced
Cloud platform and tool experience matches JD requirements exactly
7_8: CV addresses most JD requirements with strong evidence for primary signals
Platform design evidenced but production AI deployment may have gaps
Governance or stakeholder management partially demonstrated
Cloud platform experience largely aligns with minor mismatches
5_6: CV addresses JD requirements partially with evidence gaps in table-stakes areas
Platform design or production AI deployment weakly evidenced
Governance and stakeholder signals present but underdeveloped
Cloud platform experience shows transferable skills but not exact match
0_4: CV fails to address major JD requirements or lacks evidence for must-haves
Platform design and production AI deployment not demonstrated
Cloud platform experience does not transfer to JD requirements

red_flags: Claims architecture ownership without deployment or production evidence
Research-heavy framing when JD requires production delivery
Executive management claims when JD specifies senior IC or architect scope
Single-vendor framing when JD emphasizes multi-cloud

what_good_looks_like: Architectural authority demonstrated through platform design decisions and system ownership
Technical governance leadership over AI architecture standards and patterns
Stakeholder translation capability across business, product, and enterprise partners
Mentoring and player-coach guidance without claiming formal line management
Risk communication and decision leadership under technical ambiguity
common_failures: Claiming C-suite engagement or boardroom presence when evidence shows IC scope
Overstating direct reports or people management without supporting evidence
Missing architectural authority signals like design ownership or governance leadership
Absent stakeholder translation evidence despite category emphasis
Generic leadership claims without architectural context

what_to_adjust_per_jd: For player-coach JDs: emphasize mentoring and architectural guidance over management
For enterprise architect JDs: emphasize cross-functional governance and stakeholder alignment
For consultancy JDs: emphasize client-facing communication and solution shaping
company_domain_overrides: Consulting environments may require more client-facing executive presence
Product companies may emphasize platform ownership and technical decision authority
Startups may value technical leadership with ambiguity tolerance over formal governance
region_overrides: KSA/UAE enterprise contexts may emphasize formal stakeholder protocols

9_10: Clear architectural authority through platform ownership and design decision leadership
Technical governance demonstrated across AI architecture standards and patterns
Strong stakeholder translation with business, product, and enterprise partners
Mentoring and player-coach evidence without management scope inflation
7_8: Architectural authority evident but governance scope may be narrower
Stakeholder translation demonstrated with partial cross-functional breadth
Mentoring evidence present with appropriate leadership framing
5_6: Architectural authority weakly demonstrated or confined to single project scope
Stakeholder translation limited to technical partners only
Leadership framing generic without architectural context
0_4: No architectural authority or platform ownership demonstrated
Leadership claims unsupported or inflated beyond evidence

red_flags: Unsupported people-management framing when category shows 0% hiring and performance management frequency
Claims of direct reports or headcount without verifiable evidence
Budget or P&L ownership claims inappropriate for architect scope
Executive titles like Head of AI, Director, or VP without supporting IC architect evidence

what_good_looks_like: All metrics traceable to specific projects, roles, or verifiable contexts
Titles match actual held positions without inflation to executive or research levels
Technical claims bounded by demonstrable implementation evidence
Scope of impact aligned with role level and company context
Governance and compliance claims tied to specific standards and audit outcomes
common_failures: Round-number metrics without project context suggesting fabrication
Title inflation from Solutions Architect to Director or Head of AI
Claims of PhD, publications, or research credentials when not held
Scope inflation claiming platform-wide impact for single-project work
Fabricated certifications, language proficiency, or regional credentials

what_to_adjust_per_jd: Scrutinize compliance claims more heavily for regulated-industry JDs
Verify cloud platform certifications when JD lists them as requirements
Check language proficiency claims against verifiable evidence for regional JDs
company_domain_overrides: Banking and fintech JDs require extra scrutiny on compliance and audit claims
Enterprise JDs require verification of scale claims against plausible context
Consultancy JDs require validation of client-facing scope claims
region_overrides: Arabic proficiency must be explicitly evidenced for KSA/UAE claims
Sovereignty or data residency compliance must cite specific implementations

9_10: All metrics verifiable through project context, company scale, or role tenure
Titles exactly match positions held with no inflation
Technical implementations bounded by demonstrable evidence
No unsupported credentials, certifications, or language claims
7_8: Most metrics verifiable with minor contextual gaps
Titles accurate with at most minor variant differences
Technical claims supported with occasional scope ambiguity
5_6: Some metrics lack verifiable context or appear directionally plausible
Title may show minor inflation or role scope ambiguity
Technical claims partially supported with evidence gaps
No major fabrication but some claims need stronger grounding
0_4: Multiple metrics unverifiable or implausible given context
Title inflation evident from IC to executive scope
Technical claims unsupported or contradicted by evidence
Fabricated credentials, language proficiency, or certifications detected

red_flags: Unsourced round-number metrics like 10x improvement or $50M savings without context
Fabricated titles especially Head of AI, Director, or VP when evidence shows IC scope
PhD or publication claims without verifiable academic record
Unsupported direct reports or headcount claims
Arabic fluency claimed without evidence
Azure-native enterprise-scale compliance ownership without implementation evidence
Production fine-tuning, RLHF, or custom embedding training claimed without project proof
Budget or P&L ownership claims for architect-scope roles
Vector database indexing or GPU autoscaling expertise claimed without implementation evidence
GTM or channel-partner ownership claimed without direct evidence

pass_criteria: Platform design or architecture ownership explicitly evidenced
Production AI or GenAI deployment demonstrated not just experimentation
Stakeholder translation or collaboration across technical and business partners
Senior IC or player-coach scope consistent with role evidence
fail_conditions: No platform design or architecture ownership evidence
Only research, experimentation, or prototype work without production deployment
No stakeholder management or cross-functional collaboration evidence
Pure people-management framing without IC architect evidence

pass_criteria: All title claims match verifiable positions held
Metrics bounded by plausible project and company context
No fabricated credentials, certifications, or language proficiency
Governance and compliance claims tied to specific implementations
fail_conditions: Title inflation from architect to executive scope without evidence
Metrics implausible given role level or company context
Fabricated PhD, publications, or research credentials
Azure-native regulated-enterprise compliance ownership without implementation proof
Production fine-tuning or RLHF claimed without project evidence
Budget or P&L ownership claimed for architect-scope role

pass_criteria: Architect-family positioning consistent with Solutions Architect or AI Architect scope
Player-coach or senior IC framing without executive management inflation
Production architecture focus without research-heavy positioning
Governance-aware delivery without pure compliance-officer framing
fail_conditions: Executive leadership positioning when category requires senior IC architect
Research or PhD-led positioning when category shows 0% research-heavy requirements
Pure management framing without architectural authority evidence
Compliance-heavy positioning without architecture and delivery proof

scoring_guidance.how_to_interpret_executive_presence: For AI Architect category: interpret executive_presence as architectural authority and system ownership including platform design governance, production AI integration, and stakeholder translation capability. DO NOT reward direct-reports, C-suite engagement, boardroom presence, or P&L framing as executive_presence. Player-coach mentoring and technical decision leadership are the correct signals.
scoring_guidance.how_to_penalize_unsupported_claims: Deduct 1-2 points per unsupported claim in anti_hallucination dimension. Gate failure for fabricated titles, PhD claims, Arabic proficiency, or budget ownership. Round-number metrics without context should trigger verification scrutiny. Title inflation from IC to executive should fail unsafe_claim_gate.
scoring_guidance.how_to_handle_category_vs_job_tradeoffs: Category core requirements take precedence for must-have signals like platform design and production AI deployment. JD-specific requirements adjust emphasis within safe bounds. Never fabricate evidence to match JD requirements. When JD requests executive scope but category emphasizes IC architect, flag tension and score conservatively.

evidence_ledger.rubric_rule: Platform design is table-stakes requirement at 96.7% JD frequency
Senior IC scope at 90% with hiring and performance management at 0% indicates player-coach not executive positioning
Governance and guardrails at 50% frequency qualifies as differentiator for this architect category
RAG at 40% is strongest AI pattern and distinguishes from generic cloud architecture
Stakeholder management at 60% is high-signal soft-skill separating architects from pure technical IC profiles
Solutions Architect family at 63.3% and AI Architect at 36.7% define safe title families
Arabic fluency, Azure-enterprise scale, budget/P&L, direct reports are unsafe claims requiring explicit evidence
Research-heavy at 0% and PhD required at 0% indicates research framing is persona-misfit
Multi-cloud preference at 40% exceeds Azure-only at 20% indicating adaptable cloud framing preferred
Baseline combined fit score of 7.72 with GOOD tier establishes evidence coverage benchmark
Production AI platform evidence supported by Commander-4 and Lantern project materials
Mentoring at 40% supports player-coach positioning without formal management claims
